texto: Government agencies are trying to get doctors to cut back on prescribing opioids. I understand that they need to do something about the epidemic of overdoses. However, labeling everyone as addicts, including those who responsibly take opioids for chronic pain, is not the answer. If the proposed changes take effect, they would force physicians to neglect their patients. Moreover, legitimate pain patients, like myself, would be left in agony on a daily basis.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alison Moore: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “Government agencies are trying to get doctors to cut back on prescribing opioids. I understand th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, addiction, addicts pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e addicts.

frase_original: Government agencies are trying to get doctors to cut back on prescribing opioids. I understand that they need to do something about the epidemic of overdoses. However, labeling everyone as addicts, including those who responsibly take opioids for chronic pain, is not the answer. If the proposed changes take effect, they would force physicians to neglect their patients. Moreover, legitimate pain patients, like myself, would be left in agony on a daily basis.
